summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. All 13 review-request items PASS. Backend: 7/7 new tests for /api/ai/help (auth gating, Socratic first-turn behavior, follow-up, history filters starts-only, daily-limit 402) plus 40/40 regression — 47/47 total. Frontend: GlobalNav present on landing, login, register, pricing, dashboard, subjects, focus, progress, /help. Microsoft button removed from login + register UI. /help page renders Socratic form; submitting 'Solve 2(x-3)=14' returned a question-mark response that did NOT contain x=10. Follow-up + reset buttons work. Logged-in nav shows Home, Classes, Homework Help, Plans, Focus, Progress, user name + Sign out.
action_items: (Carry-over, optional) Split server.py (~960 lines) into routers (auth, ai, billing, focus, progress) for maintainability.
(Carry-over, optional) Standardise checkout invalid-plan to 400 (currently 422 due to Pydantic Literal).

(Nit) /api/ai/help: rate counter combines generated_content + chat_messages, so a single free user share daily quota across explanation/quiz/help — intentional, but worth documenting on the Pricing page.
critical_code_review_comments: Socratic system prompt is well-constructed: explicit 'NEVER give final answer on first turn', mandatory diagnostic question, calibrated to grade level. Verified live on 'Solve 2(x-3)=14' — model asks 'which part are you stuck on?' and does NOT reveal x=10.
/api/ai/help correctly returns 400 when session_id is provided but message is missing — prevents accidental no-op LLM calls.
/api/ai/help/history filters {problem: {$ne: null}} so only session starts are listed, hiding follow-up turns. Matches spec.
Daily limit logic in /api/ai/help correctly sums both generated_content + chat_messages today — prevents users from bypassing the cap by switching modes.

GlobalNav: links array conditional on `user`; both desktop & mobile menus rendered. data-testid coverage is comprehensive (nav-home, nav-classes, nav-help, nav-plans, nav-focus, nav-progress, globalnav-user-link, globalnav-logout, plus -m mobile variants).

Login.jsx and Register.jsx: Microsoft button fully removed (confirmed via UI scan — no element matching button:has-text('Microsoft')). Backend /api/auth/microsoft endpoint kept (returns 503) — acceptable for forward-compat as noted.
Help.jsx: clean error handling, 402 routes user to /pricing with toast action. Loading state visible. Reset button correctly clears problem/messages/sessionId/input.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on functional checks (19/20 testid checks; 1 false negative on logout URL match — screenshot confirms logged-out nav rendered correctly)

test_credentials: Backend: dynamically created TEST_help_* / TEST_helphist_* / TEST_helplimit_* via /api/auth/register. Frontend smoke user: ui_iter3_<timestamp>@scholarhub.app / StudyHard2026!.
seed_data_creation: test_daily_limit_returns_402 seeds 5 chat_messages docs (mode=homework_help) dated today for a fresh user, then asserts 402 on next /api/ai/help call.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three pytest suites totalling 47 tests, ~117s run: /app/backend/tests/test_scholarhub_backend.py (iter1, 26), test_billing_papers.py (iter2, 14), test_homework_help.py (iter3, 7). All pass. Server is now 959 lines — splitting recommended. /api/auth/microsoft is intentionally kept but Microsoft UI buttons are removed; backend returns 503 when MS_CLIENT_ID empty. /api/ai/help system prompt is the safety contract: any change must preserve 'NEVER give final answer on first turn'. To verify Socratic behavior live, post 'Solve 2(x-3)=14' with grade_level=high_school — first response must contain '?' and must NOT contain 'x = 10'.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All review-request items pass.
